---
title: "CV"
description: "Curriculum vitae."
---

## Curriculum vitae

Place your CV file in the root folder as `cv.pdf`, then this button will work:

[Download CV](cv.pdf){.btn .btn-primary}

## Short academic profile

**Sahil Imtiyaz**  
Researcher working on mathematical structures for evolving biological, physical, and communication systems.

## Research areas

- mathematical biology;
- complex systems;
- applied topology and geometry;
- evolving networks;
- local-to-global compatibility and obstruction methods;
- multi-level biological organization;
- molecular and biological communication.

## Education

## Education

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### PhD in Sciences and Technology: Computer Science

**University of Camerino, Italy**  
**Defended:** 29 November 2023  
**Doctoral programme:** Sciences and Technology — Computer Science  

**Thesis:** *Contextual Semantics Machinery*  

My doctoral research developed mathematical and computational structures for contextual semantics, with emphasis on quantum contextuality, local-to-global obstruction, topology-driven computation, and mathematical modelling of complex systems.

**Evaluation:** The doctoral evaluation judged the research to be original and of very high quality, with specific reference to quantum contextuality and its interconnections with topology and cohomological algebra.

**Evaluation committee and experts:** Alessandra Di Pierro, Ugo Dal Lago, Alessandro Della Corte, Giuseppe Longo, Serafin Rodrigues, and Rui Soares Barbosa.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### MS in Computer Science

**International Islamic University Islamabad, Pakistan**  
**Completed:** 2018  

**Thesis:** *Quantum Adiabatic Evolution for Global Optimization in Big Data*  

**Evaluation:** 3.86 / 4.0  

This thesis studied quantum adiabatic evolution and global optimization in the context of complex data problems.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology

**Model Institute of Engineering and Technology, Jammu, India**  
**University of Jammu**  
**Completed:** 2014  

**Result:** First Division  

Undergraduate degree in Information Technology Engineering, Faculty of Engineering, University of Jammu.

:::
:::
:::

:::


## Teaching and Supervision

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Course Tutoring and Teaching Support

**Theory of Computation and Distributed Coordination Calculus**  
Course tutoring / teaching-assistant support during doctoral studies in the Computer Science and Mathematics doctoral programme at the University of Camerino, under the academic supervision of Prof. Emanuela Merelli.

**Artificial Intelligence and Data Mining Principles**  
Master’s-level teaching support in computer science, connected to artificial intelligence, data mining, and computational methods.  
Associated academic context: Prof. Jamal Abdul Nasir.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### School-Level Teaching

**Physics and Mathematics**  
Teaching experience at higher-secondary level, with emphasis on mathematical foundations, physics concepts, and laboratory-based learning.

:::
:::
:::

:::

## Skills

- mathematical modeling;
- theoretical framework development;
- topology, geometry, and network methods;
- scientific writing and manuscript preparation;
- interdisciplinary research across biology, physics, and computation.
